evidence: Protocol namespace/action tokens are defined in `goseg/protocol/actionids/actionids.go` (lines 4-11), then re-wrapped in `goseg/protocol/contracts/protocol_contracts.go` (lines 11-17), and recomposed again into contract IDs with string concatenation (lines 25-27), creating multiple boundary representations for the same identity.

Cross-module contract identity strings are also hand-authored in tests (`goseg/protocol/conformance/fixtures_test.go:33`, `goseg/protocol/contracts/family_specs_test.go:65`, `goseg/protocol/contracts/family_specs_test.go:83`) rather than derived from a single canonical builder, increasing blast radius for naming/schema changes.

Governance ownership is encoded as free-form module path strings in multiple families (`goseg/protocol/contracts/protocol_contracts.go:43,55,67` and `goseg/protocol/contracts/startram_contracts.go:28`), which couples architecture metadata to string literals without compile-time boundary guarantees.

findings.summary: Protocol action identity is duplicated across packages and tests, indicating a missing single boundary for contract naming.
evidence: `protocol_contracts.go:11-17` re-declares these as new typed constants and `protocol_contracts.go:25-27` rebuilds IDs by string formula.
Tests still embed contract path literals directly (`fixtures_test.go:33`, `family_specs_test.go:65`, `family_specs_test.go:83`), so identity changes require edits across multiple modules.
findings.suggestion: Introduce one canonical contract identity source (e.g., typed builders for namespace/action and contract ID) and make both production specs and tests consume it; reserve raw literals only for explicit malformed-input tests generated from canonical values (e.g., canonical + invalid suffix).

findings.summary: Contract governance ownership is tracked via unconstrained module-name strings, weakening boundary integrity across modules.
evidence: Governance owner uses raw literals (`OwnerModule: "uploadsvc"`, `"system/wifi"`) in `protocol_contracts.go:43,55,67`.
A different family repeats the same pattern (`OwnerModule: "startram"`) in `startram_contracts.go:28`.
Tests rely on governance metadata construction patterns but cannot verify owner identity against a typed boundary because owner is plain string (`family_specs_test.go:18,55,80`).
findings.suggestion: Replace `OwnerModule` free-form strings with a typed module identifier enum/registry and validation helpers; require contract specs to use those typed identifiers so refactors and ownership changes are enforced centrally.
